model_narrative: We plan how much of each product class to make and sell before we know how much labor each unit will actually require. The labor requirements and the labor already on hand vary across many equally likely scenarios. After production is fixed, in each scenario we may buy extra labor at each workstation to cover any shortfall, at a known cost. We want to choose the production amounts that make the expected profit across all scenarios as large as possible.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the product classes that can be made and sold

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the equally likely scenarios for the uncertain labor data; there are 300 of them

params.name: c
params.doc: profit earned per unit of a product class sold, in dollars

params.name: q
params.doc: cost of purchasing one unit of extra labor at a workstation, in dollars

params.name: t
params.doc: labor hours required at a workstation to make one unit of a product class under a given scenario

params.name: h
params.doc: labor hours already available at a workstation under a given scenario

vars.name: EProfit
vars.doc: the expected profit across all scenarios, in dollars

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the amount of each product class produced and sold, a first-stage decision common to every scenario

vars.name: v
vars.doc: the amount of extra labor purchased at a workstation under a given scenario

constraints.name: s)
constraints.intent: Define the expected profit. It equals the total sales profit from production, which is the per-unit profit of each product class times the amount of that class produced summed over all classes, minus the average labor purchasing cost across all scenarios. That average is the cost of extra labor at each workstation times the extra labor bought there, summed over every workstation and scenario, divided by the number of scenarios. Set the expected profit variable equal to this value.

constraints.name: x[i]
constraints.intent: Production of every product class must be nonnegative. For each product class, the amount produced and sold must be at least zero.

constraints.name: t[j,
constraints.intent: Labor must balance at every workstation in every scenario. For each workstation and scenario, the total labor needed to make the chosen production, which is the labor required per unit times the amount produced summed over all product classes, must not exceed the labor already available there plus the extra labor purchased there in that scenario.

query: There's a proposal to hold total monthly output across the whole catalog to no more than 1200 units while we shake out a downstream packaging bottleneck. If we hold combined production at or under 1200, what does the optimizer return for expected profit?
expected_pyomo: model.total_output_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[i] for i in model.i) <= 1200)
